2603.28416.title: Evolutionary Discovery of Reinforcement Learning Algorithms via Large Language Models
2603.28416.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning algorithms are defined by their learning update rules, which are typically hand-designed and fixed. We present an evolutionary framework for discovering reinforcement learning algorithms by searching directly over executable update rules that implement complete training procedures. The approach builds on REvolve, an evolutionary system that uses large language models as generative variation operators, and extends it from reward-function discovery to algorithm discovery. To promote the emergence of nonstandard learning rules, the search excludes canonical mechanisms such as actor--critic structures, temporal-difference losses, and value bootstrapping. Because reinforcement learning algorithms are highly sensitive to internal scalar parameters, we introduce a post-evolution refinement stage in which a large language model proposes feasible hyperparameter ranges for each evolved update rule. Evaluated end-to-end by full training runs on multiple Gymnasium benchmarks, the discovered algorithms achieve competitive performance relative to established baselines, including SAC, PPO, DQN, and A2C.
2603.28416.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習演算法的定義依賴於其學習更新規則，這些規則通常是手動設計且固定的。<lb/>我們提出了一個進化框架，通過直接搜索可執行的更新規則來發現強化學習演算法，這些規則實現了完整的訓練過程。<lb/>該方法基於REvolve，一個進化系統，利用大型語言模型作為生成變異運算子，並將其從獎勵函數發現擴展到演算法發現。<lb/>為了促進非標準學習規則的出現，搜索排除了典型機制，如行為者-評論者結構、時間差損失和價值自助法。<lb/>由於強化學習演算法對內部標量參數高度敏感，我們引入了一個進化後的精煉階段，在此階段中，大型語言模型為每個演變的更新規則提出可行的超參數範圍。<lb/>通過在多個Gymnasium基準上進行完整的訓練運行進行端到端評估，所發現的演算法相對於已建立的基準（包括SAC、PPO、DQN和A2C）達到了具有競爭力的性能。
2603.28416.author: Alkis Sygkounas et.al.
2603.28416.authors: Alkis Sygkounas, Amy Loutfi, Andreas Persson
